claims.evidence_text: the seedling development stage (1) takes place and is characterised by leaf formation. About one leaf is produced every four to five days, usually up to nine leaves in total.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat produces about one leaf every 4-5 days during seedling development, up to nine leaves total.

claims.evidence_text: Three tillers per plant are frequently produced but certain varieties can produce more (up to nine).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat frequently produces three tillers per plant, with some varieties producing up to nine.

claims.evidence_text: winter wheat grown in a greenhouse requires six to eight months to finish one seed to seed cycle (including a vernalization period of 30 to 60 days). A spring variety takes three to four months as it does not require vernalization.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat requires a vernalization period of 30-60 days; spring varieties require none.

claims.evidence_text: Some varieties are called facultative because they only need a shorter vernalization period (15 to 30 days) with temperatures varying from 3 to 15 °C.
claims.claim_summary: Facultative wheat varieties need a shorter vernalization period of 15-30 days at temperatures of 3-15 C.

claims.evidence_text: facultative... only need a shorter vernalization period (15 to 30 days) with temperatures varying from 3 to 15 °C.
claims.claim_summary: Effective vernalization temperature range for facultative wheat is 3-15 C.

claims.evidence_text: winter wheat grown in a greenhouse requires six to eight months to finish one seed to seed cycle (including a vernalization period of 30 to 60 days). A spring variety takes three to four months.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat seed-to-seed cycle is about six to eight months (180-240 days) in greenhouse; spring varieties three to four months.

claims.evidence_text: Hard dough stage represents the attainment of maximum dry weight with approximately 30 % moisture content. This stage is also known as physiological maturity.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat reaches physiological maturity (hard dough, max dry weight) at approximately 30 percent grain moisture.

claims.evidence_text: the seed moisture will decrease down to 13 to 14 %. This is the ripening stage (9) and the seed may be harvested at the end of this stage.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat seed moisture decreases to 13-14 percent at ripening, when grain may be harvested.

claims.evidence_text: Milk development stage (7)... Kernel size then increases rapidly during this stage which occurs one to two weeks after pollination. The dough development stage (8) is the stage during which most of the kernel dry weight (starch and protein content) accumulates.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain filling spans milk (rapid kernel size increase 1-2 weeks after pollination) through dough development when most dry weight accumulates.

claims.evidence_text: Anthesis is physiologically one of the most sensitive stages and highly affected by temperature.
claims.claim_summary: Anthesis (flowering) is one of the most physiologically temperature-sensitive wheat growth stages.
